title: The Siege of Thebes
summary: CME00051 is the General Corpus XML representation of John Lydgate's Middle English Siege of Thebes. The repository artifact copies the University of Michigan encoding of Axel Erdmann's 1911 EETS text. Erdmann based the text on British Library Arundel MS 119 and collated twenty additional manuscripts plus the c. 1497 Wynkyn de Worde and 1561 John Stow prints. This graph keeps the work, repository artifact, digital encodings, bibliographically distinct 1911 issues, companion 1930 apparatus, twenty-one collated manuscripts, early prints, later modern edition, facsimiles, access routes, and rights layers separate. Where a public whole-manuscript surrogate is available, the Git-ignored cache retains the complete provider sequence and maps the work's folios and canvases inside it; selected-image objects remain explicitly partial.
review_notes: Erdmann's printed key, not intuitive siglum expansion, controls the twenty-one manuscript and two early-print source identities.

The cached 1911 volume has a Chaucer Society title page ('1911, for the Issue of 1906') while the U-M title page identifies the EETS/Oxford University Press issue. They are distinct issues linked as versions, not collapsed into one object.
Current scholarship reports thirty-one manuscripts. Witnesses identified after Erdmann's work belong to the broader tradition but are not added to his historical collation chain.
The current schema's scholarly_edition entity type is used provisionally for the c.1497 and 1561 printed editions; it records a printed textual layer and does not imply modern critical editing.

The Yale and Trinity whole-object caches contain every canvas exposed by their provider manifests. Their work_portion fields point to the narrower Siege loci, and their notes state which physical exterior views the providers do not expose.
Underlying medieval and early-print texts, modern editorial content, digital markup, manuscript photography, catalog metadata, and provider contracts receive separate rights treatment.

entities.label: General Corpus source XML for CME00051
entities.description: The XML artifact consumed by the General Corpus publication workflow and supplied through the Kyvero-Press/CME git submodule.
notes: IDG/@ID, BIBNO, and VID all identify CME00051.
TEMPHEAD records a final review on 2016-08-05.

entities.label: University of Michigan Corpus of Middle English item CME00051
entities.description: Public electronic text encoding Erdmann's title page and the full edited poem on printed pages 1-193, including marginal glosses, textual notes, page milestones, and Arundel leaf milestones.

entities.label: Axel Erdmann, Lydgate's Siege of Thebes, Part I: The Text (EETS e.s. 108, 1911)
entities.description: The immediate source edition represented by U-M: an Arundel 119 base text with marginal summaries and variants from twenty other manuscripts and the two oldest printed editions.

bibliographic.citation: John Lydgate, Lydgate's Siege of Thebes, edited from all the known manuscripts and the two oldest editions, with introduction, notes, and a glossary, by Axel Erdmann, Part I: The Text, EETS Extra Series 108 (London: Kegan Paul, Trench, Trübner & Co. for the Early English Text Society; Oxford University Press, 1911).
bibliographic.title: Lydgate's Siege of Thebes. Part I.—The Text
bibliographic.series: Early English Text Society, Extra Series 108
bibliographic.publisher: Kegan Paul, Trench, Trübner & Co. and Oxford University Press for the Early English Text Society

notes: The exact EETS issue was identified from the encoded title page. The cached Chaucer Society issue preserves the same text pagination but has different issue matter.

entities.label: Axel Erdmann, Lydgate's Siege of Thebes, Chaucer Society issue (1911 for 1906)
entities.description: The Chaucer Society issue of Erdmann's Part I text represented by the cached Warburg PDF. Its title page identifies 1911 as the date and calls it the issue for 1906.

bibliographic.citation: John Lydgate, Lydgate's Siege of Thebes, ed. Axel Erdmann, Chaucer Society, Second Series 46 (London, 1911, for the issue of 1906).
bibliographic.title: Lydgate's Siege of Thebes. Part I.—The Text

entities.label: Axel Erdmann and Eilert Ekwall, Lydgate's Siege of Thebes, Part II (EETS e.s. 125, 1930)
entities.description: The posthumously completed companion volume containing introduction, manuscript descriptions, notes, rhyme lists, glossary, and appendix. Ekwall completed it from Erdmann's drafts after Erdmann's death.

bibliographic.citation: Lydgate's Siege of Thebes, Part II: Introduction, Notes, Rhyme-Lists, and a Glossary with an Appendix, by Axel Erdmann and Eilert Ekwall, EETS Extra Series 125 (London: Humphrey Milford, Oxford University Press, 1930).
bibliographic.title: Lydgate's Siege of Thebes. Part II
bibliographic.series: Early English Text Society, Extra Series 125
bibliographic.publisher: Humphrey Milford, Oxford University Press for the Early English Text Society

entities.label: METS digital edition of The Siege of Thebes
entities.description: A current open scholarly edition with introduction, witness census, source study, and a text freshly checked against Arundel 119. It is a later comparison edition, not an ancestor of CME00051.

entities.label: British Library Arundel MS 119
entities.description: Erdmann's base manuscript, an early and complete copy of the Siege written about 1430 and occupying the codex alone. The text ends on f.79r; the volume has 80 vellum leaves.

holding.notes: Erdmann siglum Ar; formerly British Museum, Bibliotheca Arundeliana 119.

notes: The sole work in the codex; written for William de la Pole according to current scholarship.

entities.label: Cambridge University Library MS Add. 6864
entities.description: A 75-folio paper copy of the Siege, dated by its paper stock to the 1450s-1460s. The work occupies the manuscript; the public digital object exposes only MSI images of ff.5r and 8r.

holding.notes: Erdmann siglum G; the historical Gurney manuscript is now CUL Add. 6864.

entities.label: Bodleian Library MS Bodley 776
entities.description: An early parchment copy of The Siege of Thebes, dated c. 1430-1440. Digital Bodleian currently publishes a single selected slide image of f.1r, not a whole-codex surrogate.

entities.label: Trinity College Cambridge MS O.5.2
entities.description: A fifteenth-century parchment composite containing Generydes, Lydgate's Troy Book, and an acephalous Siege of Thebes on ff.191r-211v.

notes: The provider sequence includes all three works and surrounding physical matter.

entities.label: Former Prince Frederick Duleep Singh manuscript, Old Buckenham Hall
entities.description: The manuscript used under Erdmann's siglum Du. Current scholarship lists the former Duleep Singh/Old Buckenham witness as untraced; a present repository and shelfmark have not been established.

entities.label: Bodleian Library MS Laud Misc. 557
entities.description: A second-half fifteenth-century manuscript containing The Siege of Thebes as its principal or sole text; no public whole-codex surrogate was found.

entities.label: Lambeth Palace Library MS 742
entities.description: A fifteenth-century manuscript of The Siege of Thebes used by Erdmann under siglum Lb. No public whole-manuscript digital object was verified; research and reproduction images are orderable from Lambeth Palace Library.

entities.label: Yale, Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, MS 661
entities.description: A complete parchment copy of the Siege on ff.1r-61v, copied by Stephen Doddesham and dated 1425-1450. Yale identifies the manuscript as completely digitized.

holding.notes: Erdmann siglum M; the former Mostyn manuscript is now Beinecke 661.

entities.label: Bodleian Library MS Digby 230
entities.description: A third-quarter fifteenth-century composite containing The Siege of Thebes and Lydgate's Troy Book; no public digital facsimile was found.

entities.label: British Library Add MS 18632
entities.description: A fifteenth-century vellum composite in which The Story of Thebes begins on f.1 and Hoccleve's Regement of Princes begins on f.30; older household-account leaves survive as wrappers.

notes: The Siege begins f.1; Hoccleve begins f.30, so the work locus ends before f.30 but was not narrowed further here.

entities.label: British Library Add MS 5140
entities.description: A c. 1485-1500 paper-and-parchment Canterbury Tales manuscript followed by an imperfect Siege of Thebes on ff.359v-425v.

entities.label: British Library Egerton MS 2864
entities.description: A late-fifteenth-century Canterbury Tales composite with an imperfect Siege on ff.292v-341r, missing leaves after ff.335 and 340.

notes: Missing ll.3935-4117 after f.335 and ll.4582-4678 after f.340 according to the current catalog.

entities.label: Bodleian Library MS Rawl. C. 48
entities.description: A late-fifteenth-century composite codex whose Siege occupies ff.5r-78r. Digital Bodleian publishes one selected slide image of the opening f.5r, not a whole-codex surrogate.

entities.label: Longleat House MS 257
entities.description: A vellum composite whose collation accounts for 212 leaf positions, including 28 lost leaves; The Siege of Thebes occupies ff.1r-48v and is followed by other Middle English works. No authorized public complete facsimile was verified.

physical_description.extent: 212 collational leaf positions, including 28 lost leaves; Siege ff.1r-48v

entities.label: British Library Add MS 29729
entities.description: A paper manuscript copied chiefly by John Stow in 1558; The Siege of Thebes occupies ff.17r-83r within a larger collection of Lydgate works.

entities.label: Cambridge University Library MS Add. 3137
entities.description: A parchment copy of the Siege associated with the scribe Stephen Doddesham. Published descriptions disagree on whether the relevant surviving extent should be summarized as 48 or 61 leaves; no public whole-codex surrogate was found.

notes: Erdmann-Ekwall reports 48 leaves; modern scholarship reports 61 parchment leaves plus later matter. The discrepancy remains unresolved.

entities.label: Trinity College Cambridge MS R.4.20
entities.description: A fifteenth-century parchment composite containing Mandeville's Travels, the Siege on ff.89r-169r, hymns, and surrounding matter.

entities.label: Magdalene College, Pepys Library, MS 2011
entities.description: A manuscript copy of The Siege of Thebes collated by Erdmann under siglum P. Its exact work locus and whole-object physical extent have not yet been reverified from an item-level modern catalog.

holding.notes: Erdmann siglum P; held in the Pepys Library.

entities.label: Bodleian Library MS Laud Misc. 416
entities.description: A fifteenth-century paper composite, dated in part to 1459, in which The Siege of Thebes begins on f.227r.

physical_description.extent: At least 289 folios; Siege begins f.227r

entities.label: British Library Royal MS 18 D II
entities.description: A mid-fifteenth-century parchment composite whose Siege occupies ff.147v-162r, within a richly varied collection of Middle English literature.

date_statements.label: British Library dating for the relevant production phase

entities.label: British Library Cotton Appendix XXVII
entities.description: A second-half fifteenth-century composite whose imperfect Siege occupies ff.3r-51v, after The Fifteen Joys of Our Lady on ff.1r-2v.

entities.label: St John's College Cambridge MS 266
entities.description: The manuscript identified by current scholarship as Wynkyn de Worde's copy-text for the c. 1497 print. It was not one of Erdmann's twenty-one manuscript sigla and is collateral to his print witness E1.

entities.label: Wynkyn de Worde, The Story and Destruction of Thebes (c. 1497)
alternate_labels: Prologus Here begynneth the prologue of the storye of Thebes
entities.description: The first known printed edition, produced by Wynkyn de Worde at Westminster about 1497 and collated by Erdmann as E1. The EEBO/TCP source copy at the British Library is imperfect, lacking four leaves.

bibliographic.citation: John Lydgate, Prologus: Here begynneth the prologue of the storye of Thebes ([Westminster]: Wynkyn de Worde, [1497?]); STC 17031, ESTC S120573.
bibliographic.title: Prologus Here begynneth the prologue of the storye of Thebes

entities.label: John Stow's Siege of Thebes in The Workes of Geffrey Chaucer (1561)
entities.description: The 1561 Chaucer collected edition printed by John Kingston for John Wight, incorporating additions by John Stow and including the Siege. Erdmann collated it as E2. Modern analysis finds dependence on de Worde for about the first 400 lines and a different manuscript branch thereafter.

bibliographic.citation: The woorkes of Geffrey Chaucer, newly printed, with divers additions, edited by William Thynne with additions by John Stow (London: John Kingston for John Wight, 1561), including Lydgate's Siege and Destruction of Thebes; STC 5076, ESTC S107207.
bibliographic.title: The woorkes of Geffrey Chaucer, newly printed, with divers additions

entities.label: EEBO-TCP Phase I text A06568
entities.description: A TEI P5 keyboarded encoding of the imperfect British Library copy of de Worde's c. 1497 edition, keyed from ProQuest page images and released under CC0. The page images are not part of the CC0 text package.

entities.label: TCP A06568 generated README and element summary
entities.description: A human-readable summary generated from the TCP XML, including identifiers, availability, revision history, content counts, and links.

entities.label: Warburg Institute PDF of Erdmann's 1911 Chaucer Society issue
entities.description: A complete 228-page public PDF scan. The poem occupies printed pp.1-193, physical PDF pages 25-217; the cache retains preliminaries and all other scanned matter.

entities.label: Cambridge Digital Library MSI selection for MS Add. 6864
entities.description: The complete two-canvas selection published by the provider, showing ff.5r and 8r. It is partial coverage of the 75-folio physical manuscript and not a whole-codex facsimile.
entities.reproduction_format: IIIF Presentation 2 manifest and JPEG images

entities.label: Yale complete digital sequence for Beinecke MS 661
entities.description: The complete 128-canvas sequence exposed by Yale, including front cover, separate spine, pastedowns and end matter, ff.1r-61v, and back cover. The work occupies ff.1r-61v, canvases 5-126.
entities.reproduction_format: IIIF Presentation 3 manifest and JPEG images

entities.label: Digital Bodleian selected image of MS Bodley 776 f.1r
entities.description: A single digitized 35mm slide of the opening leaf, complete relative to the provider's selected-image object but partial relative to the physical codex.
entities.reproduction_format: IIIF Presentation 2 manifest and JPEG image

entities.label: Digital Bodleian selected image of MS Rawl. C. 48 f.5r
entities.description: A single digitized 35mm slide of the Siege opening, complete relative to the selected-image object but partial relative to the physical codex.
entities.reproduction_format: IIIF Presentation 2 manifest and JPEG image

entities.label: Trinity complete IIIF sequence for MS O.5.2
entities.description: All 430 canvases exposed by Trinity, from front cover through back cover and including every leaf of the composite codex. The Siege occupies ff.191r-211v, canvases 385-426.
entities.reproduction_format: IIIF Presentation 2 manifest and JPEG images

entities.label: Trinity complete IIIF sequence for MS R.4.20
entities.description: All 182 canvases exposed by Trinity, including covers, inside covers, flyleaves, and ff.1r-172v, mostly photographed as two-page openings. The Siege occupies ff.89r-169r, provider canvases 94-174.
entities.reproduction_format: IIIF Presentation 2 manifest and JPEG images

entities.label: Jordi Sánchez-Martí, Longleat House MS 257: A Description (2005)
entities.description: A scholarly codicological and contents study supporting the 212-position collation (including 28 lost leaves) and the Siege locus ff.1r-48v.

bibliographic.citation: Jordi Sánchez-Martí, 'Longleat House MS 257: A Description,' Atlantis 27.1 (June 2005): 79-89.
bibliographic.title: Longleat House MS 257: A Description

entities.label: Folger digital images of STC 5076 copy 1
entities.description: Folger's catalog links a digital-image object for its 1561 copy, but a Turnstile challenge prevented auditing page count, coverage, direct files, IIIF, or item-level image rights. Coverage therefore remains unknown.

agents.name: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University

object.description: Part I title page and printed pp.1-193

scope.notes: The U-M XML excludes the edition's prefatory manuscript list and other preliminaries but retains the complete poem, marginal glosses, and apparatus on numbered text pages.
The issues share Erdmann's text setting and numbered pagination but have distinct title-page/series issue matter; exact plate history was not independently documented.

subject.description: Introduction, notes, rhyme lists, glossary, and appendix

object.description: 1911 edited text and its manuscript/print sources

scope.notes: The modern edition is a later comparison source and is not upstream of CME00051.
The edition explicitly heads its text with Arundel 119 and uses Ar as the base while recording emendations and variants.

object.description: Witness Bo; gaps at ll.1-8, 31-40, and 4456-4586
Witness T1; acephalous and lacking ll.615-726 and 1061-1172

assertion.notes: Erdmann siglum Du; present physical location remains unknown.

object.description: Witness Lb; gaps include ll.65-392 and 4597-4658
Witness Ad2; gaps at ll.785-788 and 4094-4158 and imperfect after l.4504

assertion.notes: Erdmann siglum I, formerly the Ingilby manuscript.

object.description: Witness Ba; gap at ll.2490-2580 and imperfect after l.4432
Witness S, John Stow's 1558 manuscript copy
Witness C; extensive losses including opening ll.1-822 and several later intervals

assertion.notes: Erdmann siglum C; the edition's loss synopsis should be consulted for every interval.

object.description: Witness T2; gaps at ll.2485-2672 and 3634-4209
Witness Ro; an imperfect copy with numerous gaps

assertion.notes: Erdmann siglum Ro; consult the edition's loss synopsis for passage-level completeness.

object.description: Witness Ap; gap at ll.1681-1746 and imperfect after l.3409
Early print E1; gaps include ll.107-176 and 4363-4418 in the surviving copy/tradition used
Siege text in the 1561 Chaucer volume

scope.notes: After about line 400 the Stow text follows a different manuscript-tradition branch; no whole-text print-to-print derivation is asserted.

object.description: Imperfect British Library copy reproduced in EEBO; four leaves lacking

scope.notes: TCP keyed the ProQuest page images. It does not supply the missing leaves or place the page images under CC0.

object.description: Bound-object sequence with ff.1r-61v and surrounding matter
Codex and its contents, including Siege ff.1r-48v
Folger STC 5076 copy 1; coverage not verified

assertion.notes: A catalog link proves some digital images, not a complete facsimile.

access.access_method: U-M item page and full-text interface
notes: Automated retrieval encountered the provider's anti-bot challenge during this review; the stable public human-facing item route remains recorded.

access.access_method: Use the public scan of the separately issued Chaucer Society version for page comparison, or request the EETS issue from a holding library.
notes: The cached public file is not silently substituted for the exact EETS title-page issue, although the text setting and printed pagination agree.

local_copies.label: Complete Erdmann 1911 Chaucer Society issue PDF

work_portion.label: Edited text of The Siege of Thebes
notes: The complete PDF retains the title page, manuscript key, preliminaries, and all matter surrounding the numbered text.

access.access_method: Google Books full-view reader and searchable text
notes: The volume was fully searchable and viewable, but direct PDF retrieval triggered a human-verification challenge. No automated bypass or local cache was attempted.

access.access_method: Open web edition
British Library digitisation service; request the complete physical manuscript and separately identify Siege ff.1r-79r.
notes: No public digital content is linked from the current item record. A complete-codex request should name covers, boards, spine, pastedowns, flyleaves, blanks, all 80 leaves, and edges when offered, while mapping the work to ff.1r-79r.

notes: Both selected leaves overlap the work, but the object omits the rest of the 75-folio manuscript and all unexposed physical surfaces.
ZIP contains the provider manifest, exact-source inventory, and both published canvas images.
Images were requested at width 1800 pixels but delivered natively at 1500 x 2000; no upscaling was forced.

local_copies.label: Trinity College Cambridge MS O.5.2 complete digital facsimile
notes: The complete 430-canvas provider inventory runs from front cover through inside covers, flyleaves, every side of ff.1-211, and back cover; Trinity exposes no separate spine or edge photographs.
ZIP contains the provider manifest, an exact-source inventory, and all 430 canvas images listed when retrieved.
Requested IIIF image profile: width 1800 pixels; JPEG. Sampled images decoded at 1800 pixels wide except the provider's front-cover response, which decoded at 1801 pixels.

work_portion.label: Lydgate's Siege of Thebes
locators: Universal Viewer zero-based cv=384 through cv=425
notes: The work follows Generydes (ff.1r-37v) and Troy Book (ff.38r-190r); the complete bundle retains both adjacent works and all provider-exposed physical matter.

access.access_method: No present holding repository is established; trace provenance before seeking reproduction.
Bodleian Imaging Services order; request the whole physical codex.
Submit Lambeth Palace Library's digital photography form, specifying MS 742 and requesting the whole physical manuscript plus a separate work locus.
access.format: High-quality TIFF/JPEG or research PDF depending order
notes: The current form lists high-quality images at £14.40 each including VAT (£12 excluding VAT) and standard microfilm-derived PDFs at £8.40 for the first image and £1.20 each thereafter, with a 40-image order limit. Whole-object feasibility and total price require a quote; publication requires a separate permission application.

access.access_method: Yale Digital Collections item and IIIF manifest

local_copies.label: Yale Beinecke MS 661 complete digital facsimile
notes: The complete provider sequence includes front cover, a separate spine canvas, front pastedown and endpaper, ff.1r-61v, back pastedown, and back cover; separate edge photographs are not exposed.
127 images were requested at width 1800 pixels; the narrow spine was retained at its native 650 x 4545 pixels without upscaling.
ZIP integrity and all 128 inventory entries were verified.

work_portion.label: The Siege of Thebes
notes: The work occupies every manuscript leaf, but the complete bundle also retains covers, spine, pastedowns, and end matter.

access.access_method: Bodleian Imaging Services order; request the whole physical codex and separately map Siege ff.5r-78r.
Contact Longleat Collections through its access page; ask whether the research-only manuscript microfilm service covers MS 257 and whether a complete digital surrogate can be supplied.
notes: The public page says PDF copies of microfilms for certain manuscripts may be supplied for research only. Coverage of MS 257 and physical-object completeness require confirmation; the Siege occupies ff.1r-48v, and a whole-object request means all extant leaves and binding matter rather than the 28 lost positions in the 212-position collation.

access.access_method: Contact the Pepys Librarian for an item record, whole-manuscript photography, exact Siege locus, and reuse terms.
notes: The Pepys Library is closed to visitors for refurbishment until 2027, but its official page continues to invite advance researcher and photographic-service enquiries.

Contact St John's College Library for item-level cataloging, exact work locus, digitization status, and whole-manuscript reproduction options.
notes: No exact public facsimile or item-level access record was verified in this review. The official page says Special Collections can supply formats ranging from private research copies to high-resolution publication images and handles reproduction permission separately.

access.access_method: EEBO/ProQuest page images of the imperfect British Library copy

notes: No complete public facsimile or authorized direct download was found. The TCP CC0 grant applies to the keyboarded text and markup, not these page images.

rights.component: U-M electronic text and displayed source materials
rights.basis: The U-M item labels the materials public domain and links the No Copyright–United States rights statement.

rights.component: Repository XML copied from the U-M encoding
rights.basis: The repository artifact is a copy of the U-M item whose current record applies No Copyright–United States; this does not grant rights in linked manuscript photographs.
The edition was published in 1911, more than 95 years before the current year; this status does not determine image-provider terms for a particular scan.

rights.component: 1911 Chaucer Society issue text and issue matter
rights.basis: Published in 1911; the historical issue content is in the U.S. public domain.

The scanned volume is public-domain U.S. material and the Warburg Institute serves the PDF publicly, but the exact scan file carries no explicit open license in the file or delivery response reviewed.

rights.component: 1930 introduction, manuscript descriptions, notes, rhyme lists, glossary, and appendix
rights.basis: A U.S. work published in 1930 reached the end of the maximum 95-year published-work term and entered the public domain on 1 January 2026; Google Books delivery terms remain separate.

rights.component: Underlying c. 1497 printed text and typography, excluding modern EEBO photographs and TCP markup
rights.basis: The early printed work predates modern copyright; EEBO access and image rights remain separate and subscription-mediated.

rights.component: Underlying 1561 printed text and typography, excluding modern photography
rights.basis: The early printed volume predates modern copyright.

rights.component: TCP keyboarded text and markup only; page images excluded
rights.jurisdiction: Worldwide dedication to the extent legally possible
rights.basis: The embedded availability statement releases the Phase I text under Creative Commons CC0 1.0 and expressly permits copying, modification, distribution, performance, and commercial use without permission.
notes: The TCP page-image links lead to Historical Texts/EEBO and are not included in the CC0 package.

rights.component: Generated README describing the CC0 TCP encoding
rights.jurisdiction: Worldwide dedication to the extent legally possible
rights.basis: The README is distributed with the pinned TCP repository and repeats the encoding's CC0 availability statement.

rights.component: The two downloadable CUDL manuscript images; metadata has a separate CC BY-NC-ND 3.0 statement
rights.basis: The exact IIIF manifest states that downloadable images are CC BY-NC 3.0 and its metadata is CC BY-NC-ND 3.0.
notes: The images may not be placed into a profit-oriented/POD corpus under this noncommercial license without additional permission. Local Git-ignored research caching does not authorize redistribution.

rights.component: Selected Digital Bodleian photograph of f.1r
rights.basis: The exact item manifest states Photo © Bodleian Libraries and applies CC BY-NC 4.0.

rights.component: Selected Digital Bodleian photograph of f.5r
rights.basis: The exact item manifest states Photo © Bodleian Libraries and applies CC BY-NC 4.0.
The exact Trinity IIIF manifest applies CC BY-NC 4.0 to the digital object.
notes: Whole-codex research caching is separate from redistribution. Commercial or profit-oriented publication requires permission beyond CC BY-NC.

rights.basis: The exact Trinity IIIF manifest applies CC BY-NC 4.0 to the digital object.
notes: Whole-codex research caching is separate from redistribution. Commercial or profit-oriented publication requires permission beyond CC BY-NC.

rights.component: Yale manuscript photographs and IIIF delivery
rights.basis: The exact IIIF manifest declares complete digitization but supplies no item-level license. Beinecke's current copyright page welcomes public-domain and fair use but places responsibility on the user, while its reproduction guidance says provision does not authorize publication.
notes: Do not publish the cached ZIP or its images in the repository without a more specific item-level permission determination; the underlying medieval text is a separate layer.

rights.basis: The institutional repository makes the PDF publicly downloadable, but no reusable open license was verified in the downloaded file or record consulted.
notes: The local hard-linked copy is research evidence and should not be redistributed as a corpus asset absent permission.

rights.component: Folger digital photographs of STC 5076 copy 1
rights.basis: Item-level image terms could not be audited through the Turnstile challenge. Folger's general image page requests source identification but does not establish the exact object's license or complete coverage.

editorial_practices.source_feature: Arundel 119 base text and readings from twenty additional manuscripts plus two early prints
editorial_practices.representation: Arundel-based edited text with marginal summaries and line-by-line footnote variants keyed to twenty-three sigla
editorial_practices.description: Erdmann's edition is not a diplomatic transcription of one codex: it uses Arundel 119 as base and records or adopts readings from a large collated tradition.

editorial_practices.source_feature: Arundel scribal flourishes for final -e and plurals, suspension marks for doubled consonants, and other abbreviations
editorial_practices.representation: Erdmann reproduces many manuscript features typographically; the XML separately marks numerous ABBR spans and italic letters, but its typography must not be read as an image of manuscript letterforms.
editorial_practices.description: The edition/encoding preserve a mixed documentary and editorial representation of abbreviations and final letters. Exact manuscript appearance requires the facsimile, not normalized XML.

editorial_practices.source_feature: Irregular or 'broken-back' manuscript lines and readings judged metrically defective
editorial_practices.representation: Erdmann intervenes more heavily than the modern METS editor to regularize heroic meter and sometimes adopts substantive corrections.
editorial_practices.description: The 1911 base text is a critical reconstruction shaped by metrical judgment, not an unaltered transcript of Arundel 119.
editorial_practices.scope: Substantive and metrical readings throughout the poem

editorial_practices.source_feature: The complete printed volume includes a manuscript key and issue preliminaries before the poem.
editorial_practices.representation: The XML includes the title page and numbered poem pp.1-193 but not the printed manuscript-key leaves and other surrounding issue matter.
editorial_practices.description: The encoding is complete for the edited poem but selective relative to the bound source edition.

editorial_practices.source_feature: ProQuest images of an imperfect British Library copy of de Worde's c.1497 print
editorial_practices.representation: TCP keyed and marked up the available pages, preserving 4,530 verse-line elements and recording 76 small illegible gaps; four physical leaves are absent from the source copy.
editorial_practices.description: The TCP file is complete as a published encoding of its imperfect source copy, not as a reconstruction of every line in de Worde's original edition.

evidence.citation: General Corpus, CME/source/CME_phase_3/CME00051.xml, from Kyvero-Press/CME commit e1d77c88ca561be924b2fedc54dd474be2e7e49c.
evidence.locator: TEMPHEAD, IDG, encoded title page, BODY/DIV1, four DIV2 units, LG/L, PB, MILESTONE, NOTE, CLOSER, and TRAILER
evidence.summary: Primary evidence for repository identity and checksum, U-M identifier family, immediate EETS title page, edited poem boundary, printed pagination and lineation, Arundel foliation, apparatus, editorial typography, and 2016 review date.

evidence.citation: Ancestor-aware XPath and checksum inspection of CME00051.xml.
evidence.locator: Counts of DIV1, DIV2, LG, L outside NOTE, NOTE/L, PB, MILESTONE, NOTE, and P; ordered line and leaf milestones
evidence.summary: Confirmed two DIV1, four textual DIV2, 4,713 main-stream L plus four apparatus L, 5 LG, 194 PB, 1,338 MILESTONE, 4,120 NOTE, and 5 P. Main-stream count offsets occur at canonical numbers 389, 1062, and 1747.

evidence.citation: University of Michigan Corpus of Middle English, CME00051 item record.
evidence.summary: Stable institutional route for the item, electronic text, and bibliographic source identity.
notes: Automated access met a Cloudflare challenge; identity and bibliographic claims were cross-checked against the local U-M-derived XML and current collection records.

evidence.citation: University of Michigan, Corpus of Middle English about page.
evidence.summary: Describes the CME expansion as manually keyed from public-domain editions with in-house proofreading and basic TEI, without establishing an item-specific keying date for CME00051.

evidence.citation: University of Michigan Corpus of Middle English, CME00051 rights statement.
evidence.summary: Marks the materials public domain and links No Copyright–United States 1.0.

evidence.citation: Axel Erdmann, ed., Lydgate's Siege of Thebes, Part I: The Text, Chaucer Society issue (London, 1911 for 1906), complete Warburg Institute PDF.
evidence.locator: PDF title page; manuscript key on physical pp.23-24; poem on physical pp.25-217 / printed pp.1-193; apparatus throughout
evidence.summary: Primary edition evidence for the Chaucer Society issue, Erdmann's editor role, twenty-one manuscript and two print sigla, Arundel base heading, printed poem, variants, lacuna synopsis, lineation, pagination, and manuscript-leaf milestones.

evidence.citation: Middle English Texts Series, The Siege of Thebes: Introduction and digital edition.
evidence.locator: Introduction, especially paragraphs on sources, composition, manuscripts, print history, Erdmann-Ekwall, and editorial method; select bibliography
evidence.summary: Current scholarly evidence for title, authorship, c.1421-1422 date, sources and genres, thirty-one-manuscript census, current shelfmarks and historical designations, de Worde/Stow transmission, Erdmann's base and collations, and distinctions between Erdmann and the modern edition.

evidence.citation: National Library of Ireland catalog, Lydgate's Siege of Thebes, Parts I-II.
evidence.summary: Catalogs the two EETS volumes as Extra Series 108 and 125, records that Part I was also issued as Chaucer Society Second Series 46, and states that Ekwall completed Part II after Erdmann's death.

evidence.citation: Google Books full-view record and searchable scan, Lydgate's Siege of Thebes, Part II, EETS Extra Series 125 (1930), volume uiOKk4m5NbwC.
evidence.locator: Bibliographic record; pp.36-56 manuscript descriptions; 220-page full-view volume
evidence.summary: Evidence for the 1930 companion volume, bibliographic facts, Ekwall's completion, manuscript physical descriptions, folio loci, and historical sigla. Direct download was blocked by human verification and was not bypassed.

evidence.citation: British Library Archives and Manuscripts Catalogue, Arundel MS 119.
evidence.summary: Exact current holding record for Arundel MS 119, supporting shelfmark, 80-leaf vellum codex identity, and current absence of linked public digital content.

evidence.citation: British Library Archives and Manuscripts Catalogue, Add MS 18632.
evidence.locator: Title, Scope & Content, includes, access, user conditions, custodial history
evidence.summary: Supports the vellum fifteenth-century composite, Siege beginning f.1, Hoccleve beginning f.30, household-account wrapper leaves, current access, and no digitised-content link.

evidence.citation: British Library Archives and Manuscripts Catalogue, Add MS 5140.
evidence.summary: Supports the c.1485-c.1500 composite, mixed paper/parchment support, Canterbury Tales contents, imperfect Siege ff.359v-425v, 425-folio extent, and current unavailability of legacy digital images.

evidence.citation: British Library Archives and Manuscripts Catalogue, Egerton MS 2864.
evidence.summary: Supports the Canterbury Tales composite, Siege locus ff.292v-341r, missing leaves after ff.335 and 340, current shelfmark, dating, and unavailable legacy digital content.

evidence.citation: British Library Archives and Manuscripts Catalogue, Add MS 29729.
evidence.summary: Supports the 1558 paper manuscript copied chiefly by John Stow, physical extent, Siege ff.17r-83r, composite Lydgate contents, and current digital-image status.

evidence.citation: British Library Archives and Manuscripts Catalogue, Royal MS 18 D II.
evidence.summary: Supports current shelfmark, parchment composite extent, Siege ff.147v-162r, mid-fifteenth-century production context, and current access/digital-image state.

evidence.citation: British Library Archives and Manuscripts Catalogue, Cotton Appendix XXVII component record.
evidence.summary: Supports the second-half fifteenth-century component, Fifteen Joys on ff.1r-2v, imperfect Siege ff.3r-51v, and absence of linked digitised content.

evidence.citation: British Library, Digitisation services.
evidence.summary: Current order route and service terms for reference or reproduction photography, including shelfmark/folio requirements, colour requests, fees, delivery, permissions, and the distinction between supplied images and authorization to publish.

evidence.citation: Cambridge Digital Library IIIF manifest, Paper stock (Cambridge, University Library, MS Additional 6864).
evidence.locator: Manifest attribution and metadata; canvases 1-2, ff.5r and 8r
evidence.summary: Exact provider evidence for shelfmark, 75-folio Siege codex, paper-stock dating, physical size, two-image-only coverage, item attribution, image CC BY-NC 3.0, and metadata CC BY-NC-ND 3.0.

evidence.citation: Cambridge Digital Library item-level IIIF attribution for MS Add. 6864 MSI selection.
evidence.summary: States that downloadable images are CC BY-NC 3.0 and metadata is CC BY-NC-ND 3.0; the claim is restricted to this exact selected-image object.

evidence.citation: Cambridge University Library, Image Supply and Reproduction.
evidence.summary: Current public order route, quote and payment process, delivery expectations, specialist-photography caveat, and separate image-use licensing workflow.

evidence.citation: Bodleian Libraries MARCO records for the Siege witnesses used by Erdmann.
evidence.summary: Exact institutional records for Bodley 776, Digby 230, Laud Misc. 557, Laud Misc. 416, and Rawl. C. 48, supporting shelfmarks, dates, supports, composite contexts, and work loci where stated.

evidence.citation: Digital Bodleian IIIF manifest, MS Bodley 776 selected f.1r image.
evidence.summary: Exact evidence that Digital Bodleian publishes one selected 35mm-slide image of f.1r, with c.1430-1440 date and CC BY-NC 4.0 item terms; it is not a whole-codex facsimile.

evidence.citation: Digital Bodleian IIIF manifest, MS Rawl. C. 48 selected f.5r image.
evidence.summary: Exact evidence that Digital Bodleian publishes one selected 35mm-slide image of the Siege opening f.5r, with late-fifteenth-century date and CC BY-NC 4.0 item terms; it is not a whole-codex facsimile.

evidence.citation: Digital Bodleian, Terms of Use.
evidence.summary: Explains item-level license checking, CC BY-NC 4.0 attribution requirements, allowed noncommercial academic uses, and prohibited profit-oriented/commercial reuse without permission.

evidence.citation: Bodleian Libraries, Order images from Imaging Services.
evidence.summary: Current high-quality and reference-copy order routes, per-image prices, large-order negotiation, form submission, delivery, and separate publication/reuse conditions.

evidence.citation: Trinity College Cambridge James Catalogue, MS O.5.2.
evidence.summary: Supports shelfmark, fifteenth-century date, parchment extent, composite contents, Siege ff.191r-211v, provenance, and complete viewer route.

evidence.citation: Trinity College Cambridge IIIF manifest for MS O.5.2.
evidence.summary: Exact source inventory for the complete provider sequence and CC BY-NC 4.0 license. Audit confirms covers, inside covers, flyleaves, every side of ff.1-211, and back cover; no separate spine or edge views.

evidence.citation: Trinity College Cambridge James Catalogue, MS R.4.20.
evidence.summary: Supports shelfmark, fifteenth-century parchment codex, 172-folio extent, composite contents, Siege ff.89r-169r, and viewer route.

evidence.citation: Trinity College Cambridge IIIF manifest for MS R.4.20.
evidence.summary: Exact source inventory for the provider sequence and CC BY-NC 4.0 license. The manifest includes covers, inside covers, flyleaves, and ff.1r-172v, mostly as two-page openings, but no separate spine or edge views.

evidence.citation: Trinity College Cambridge item-level IIIF license fields for MSS O.5.2 and R.4.20.
evidence.summary: Both exact manifests identify Trinity College Cambridge as provider and apply CC BY-NC 4.0 to their digital objects.

evidence.citation: Yale Library IIIF manifest and catalog metadata for Beinecke MS 661, object 9998988.
evidence.summary: Exact evidence for shelfmark, 1425-1450 date, England/London? origin, Stephen Doddesham, parchment extent i+61+i, complete-digitization claim, 128-canvas inventory, and work mapping.

evidence.citation: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Copyright Questions and Reproduction Orders.
evidence.summary: Current provider statements distinguish public-domain/fair use from permission, place responsibility on users, require citation, offer downloads/reproduction orders, and state that provision of a reproduction does not authorize publication. The exact MS 661 manifest supplies no license.

evidence.citation: Jordi Sánchez-Martí, 'Longleat House MS 257: A Description,' Atlantis 27.1 (June 2005): 79-89.
evidence.summary: Scholarly evidence for Longleat 257 as a vellum composite with 212 collational leaf positions, 28 of them lost, and for The Siege of Thebes on ff.1r-48v.

evidence.citation: Longleat, Access the Collections.
evidence.summary: Current institutional enquiry route and statement that PDF microfilm copies of certain manuscripts may be supplied for a fee for research only; it does not establish that MS 257 is covered or that a reel is physically complete.

evidence.citation: Lauren S. McClelland, Studies in Pre-Reformation Carthusian Book Production, PhD thesis, University of Glasgow (2013).
evidence.locator: Description of CUL MS Add. 3137 and Stephen Doddesham group
evidence.summary: Modern scholarly description of Add. 3137 as a 61-leaf parchment copy associated with Stephen Doddesham, creating a documented extent conflict with Erdmann-Ekwall's 48-leaf description.

evidence.citation: Magdalene College, Pepys Library.
evidence.summary: Current contact route for research appointments, photographic services, and general enquiries; states closure to visitors until 2027 during refurbishment.

evidence.citation: Lambeth Palace Library, Digital Photography order form and Permission to Reproduce form.
evidence.summary: Current order form, formats, prices, 40-image limit, VAT treatment, delivery, and requirement for a separate publication-permission application.

evidence.citation: Folger Shakespeare Library catalog record 410397, EEBO reproduction of STC 17031 / ESTC S120573.
evidence.summary: Supports the Westminster/Wynkyn de Worde/[1497?] imprint, identifiers, [174]-page extent, signatures, EEBO route, and the imperfect British Library source copy lacking four leaves.
notes: The record labels part of its metadata vendor supplied; current scholarly transmission claims are separately supported by METS.

evidence.citation: St John's College, Cambridge, Library and Special Collections access information.
evidence.summary: Current contact and consultation route for manuscript Special Collections; states that reading copies and high-resolution publication images can be requested and that publication or website reproduction permission is handled through special.collections@joh.cam.ac.uk.

evidence.citation: Folger Shakespeare Library catalog record 162004, STC 5076 / ESTC S107207.
evidence.summary: Supports the 1561 Chaucer volume title, Kingston/Wight imprint, Thynne and Stow roles, extent, identifiers, state/copy distinctions, and existence of a linked Folger digital-image object.

evidence.citation: Folger Shakespeare Library, Images and Permissions.
evidence.summary: Current general route for image services and requested source identification. It does not establish the exact STC 5076 object's extent or item-level license.

evidence.citation: Text Creation Partnership A06568, pinned TEI P5 XML at commit bdeb9e10053be8b5dfbef6aaf1122fba3fc7219e.
evidence.summary: Primary evidence for identifiers, c.1497 de Worde source, imperfect four-leaf loss, British Library copy, 2005 keying/review history, 4,530 verse lines, 76 illegible gaps, and CC0 license for text and markup.

evidence.citation: Text Creation Partnership A06568 generated README, pinned at commit bdeb9e10053be8b5dfbef6aaf1122fba3fc7219e.
evidence.summary: Human-readable support for availability, CC0 commercial reuse of text/markup, revision history, page-image exclusion by separate link, structural counts, and source-copy gaps.

evidence.citation: United States Copyright Office, Duration of Copyright, Circular 15A, and 17 U.S.C. chapter 3.
evidence.summary: Authoritative duration framework supporting the U.S. public-domain status of historical editions published in 1911 and 1930 as of 2026; provider contracts and modern photographs remain separate.

open_questions.question: Can the British Library supply or restore public access to a complete physical-object facsimile of Arundel MS 119, including binding matter and all 80 leaves?
next_steps: Submit a digitisation enquiry naming the complete physical object and separately mapping the Siege to ff.1r-79r.
Ask for covers or boards, spine, pastedowns, flyleaves, blanks, all leaves, and edges when safely available, plus explicit research and redistribution terms.

open_questions.question: What is the present location and survival status of the former Prince Frederick Duleep Singh / Old Buckenham Hall manuscript used by Erdmann as Du?
next_steps: Trace auction, estate, and collection provenance after the Duleep Singh holding.
Do not call the witness lost or assign a modern shelfmark without repository evidence.

open_questions.question: Why does Erdmann-Ekwall describe CUL Add. 3137 as 48 leaves while modern scholarship describes 61 parchment leaves plus later matter, and what is the exact current work locus?
next_steps: Request the current CUL item record and collation from Special Collections.
Ask any whole-object image quote to include every physical leaf and binding surface, then map the work within that delivery.

open_questions.question: What are the exact current physical extent, Siege folio range, digitization status, and image-reuse terms for Pepys MS 2011?
next_steps: Email the Pepys Librarian with Erdmann siglum P and shelfmark Pepys MS 2011.
Request an item-level catalog record before ordering a complete physical-object facsimile.

open_questions.question: What are the exact physical extent and Siege folio boundaries of Lambeth Palace Library MS 742, and can the Library quote a whole-object color reproduction?
next_steps: Submit the current photography form and ask for an item-level catalog description before accepting a page count.
Request the whole physical manuscript, not merely the work, and seek separate permission for any repository redistribution.

open_questions.question: Were the EETS Extra Series 108 and Chaucer Society Second Series 46 issues printed from identical plates throughout, and exactly which preliminaries differ?
next_steps: Compare complete physical copies or high-resolution scans of both issues page by page, including title matter, signatures, advertisements, and errata.
Retain separate edition entities until plate and issue history is documented.

open_questions.question: Does the Folger digital object for STC 5076 copy 1 reproduce the whole bound volume, and what exact image license and download routes apply?
next_steps: Audit the Folger object in a human browser session after the Turnstile challenge.
Verify canvas/image count, first and last physical surfaces, Siege page range, exact file links, and item-level rights before caching.

open_questions.question: Can any complete or selected manuscript image cache be redistributed through a repository that supports both nonprofit research and profit-oriented print-on-demand publication?
next_steps: Treat Trinity, CUDL, and Digital Bodleian CC BY-NC images as noncommercial-only and keep them Git-ignored.
Ask Yale for item-specific clarification before redistributing MS 661 images.
Obtain written commercial/repository permission where profit-oriented use is contemplated; publish links and work_portion metadata meanwhile, not cached bytes.
